chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ وَ مَا لَا يَنْقُضُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – What breaks the Ablution (Wudhu) and what does not break it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/117)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/117)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ هَلْ يَصْلُحُ لَهُ أَنْ يَسْتَدْخِلَ الدَّوَاءَ ثُمَّ يُصَلِّيَ وَ هُوَ مَعَهُ أَ يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ قَالَ لَا يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ وَ لَا يُصَلِّي حَتَّى يَطْرَحَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Al Amraky,

(It has been narrated) from Ali son of Ja’farasws, from his brotherasws Musaasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the man, ‘Is it correct for him that he inserts the medicine, then he prays Salaat and it is with him. Would it break the Ablution (Wudhu)?’ Heasws said: ‘It would not break the Ablution (Wudhu), and he should not pray Salaat until he removes it’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from al-‘Amrakiy from Ali ibn Ja‘far from his brother, Musa, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Does the medicine that one may keep inside his body during Salah (prayer), invalidate his Wudu’?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It does not invalidate his Wudu’ but he must not perform Salah (prayer) before taking it away.’”
